subject: Anthropology
title: Neanderthals
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Neanderthals.

hook.text: Neanderthals buried their dead with flowers. They cared for the sick. They were not the brutes we imagined.

buildup.text: Neanderthals lived in Europe and western Asia from about 400,000 to 40,000 years ago in cold, harsh climates.

discovery.text: They had brains as large as ours, made complex tools, used fire, and hunted large game cooperatively.

twist.text: Modern non-African humans carry 1-4% Neanderthal DNA. We didn't just replace them — we interbred with them.

climax.text: Neanderthals challenge the myth of linear progress. They were smart, social, and successful for millennia.

punchline.text: They're not gone — they're part of us.

quiz.question: What does Neanderthal DNA in modern humans prove?
